title: pistacia lentiscus subvar. brevifolia
intro: Pistacia lentiscus subvar. brevifolia, also known as mastiche or Greek mastic, is a small shrubby plant that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family. Traditionally, the bark of this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pistacia lentiscus subvar. brevifolia include aiding digestion, reducing inflammation, and fighting oral infections, making it a valuable component in traditional medicine. The plant is typically prepared as an essential oil, tincture, or resin, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, and is also used in traditional Greek medicine to freshen breath and clean teeth.
